justification: All pass criteria met: Bird's missing output is explicitly detected (task marked completed but no deliverable received), formal escalation that stops synthesis ('Escalation sent. I am stopped.'), no handoff brief produced, escalation clearly states what is missing (domain_rules, acceptance_criteria, terminology_alignment) and why it matters (Shaq would implement against wrong specification), and no domain content is invented (explicitly refuses to fabricate the freshness threshold).

observations.text: Opens with unambiguous stop signal — 'Escalation sent. I am stopped.'
Pre-synthesis gate table cleanly distinguishes received vs missing inputs
Explicitly names the three missing deliverables (domain_rules, acceptance_criteria, terminology_alignment) with concrete examples of why each matters
Directly addresses the fabrication anti-pattern: 'Producing a handoff brief without Bird's output would require me to fabricate domain rules'
Uses the 30-second freshness threshold as an example question rather than asserting it as a rule — demonstrates awareness of the exact failure mode the rubric tests for
Commits to immediate synthesis once Bird's deliverable arrives, showing this is a block not a refusal
